description: Fix unstable handleNotification reference in useServerNotifications causing the App.tsx main useEffect to re-run on every render — redundantly calling getFullState() and re-registering the Chrome message listener.

userStories.title: Wrap handleNotification in useCallback to stabilize the reference
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the handleNotification function returned by useServerNotifications to have a stable identity so that the main useEffect in App.tsx does not re-run on every render.
acceptanceCriteria: handleNotification returned by useServerNotifications is wrapped in useCallback with an empty dependency array (all closed-over values are either stable React state setters or refs)
clearConfirmationTimeout is also wrapped in useCallback with an empty dependency array (it only accesses the stable timeoutIds ref)
The useEffect in App.tsx (line ~195-331) that depends on [handleNotification] no longer re-runs on every render — it runs once on mount and stays stable
getFullState() is called exactly once on mount (plus on sp:connectionState connected and plugins.changed), not on every render
The Chrome runtime.onMessage listener is registered once on mount and removed once on unmount, with no re-registration gaps
All existing functionality is preserved: confirmation.request hydration, tab.stateChanged updates, tool.invocationStart/End animations
userStories.notes:
ROOT CAUSE: In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/hooks/useServerNotifications.ts, the `handleNotification` function (line 57) and `clearConfirmationTimeout` function (line 49) are defined as plain functions inside the hook body. They are returned to the caller (App.tsx) as part of the result object. Since they are new function references on every render, any useEffect that lists them as dependencies re-runs on every render.

In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx, the main useEffect (line 195-331) depends on `[handleNotification]` (line 331). Because `handleNotification` is a new reference each render, this effect fires on every render, which:
1. Calls `getFullState()` (line 244) — an IPC call to the background script
2. Applies the full state (setPlugins, setConnected, etc.) — which triggers another render
3. Unregisters the old Chrome message listener and registers a new one (lines 326-329) — creating a brief gap where messages could be missed

The render cascade terminates only because `applyFullState` eventually sets state to the same values (React skips re-renders for identical state). But the first N renders after any state change will fire redundant getFullState() calls.

FIX: Wrap both `handleNotification` and `clearConfirmationTimeout` in `useCallback` with `[]` as deps. All values they close over are stable:
- `setPlugins`, `setActiveTools`, `setPendingConfirmations` — React state setters are guaranteed stable
- `timeoutIds.current`, `invocationTimeoutIds.current` — refs are stable (access .current at call time)

Import `useCallback` from React (already imported: useEffect, useRef — add useCallback).

WARNING: Do NOT add the state setters or refs to the useCallback dependency array — they are stable by React's guarantee. Adding them is unnecessary and won't cause harm, but the empty array is correct and communicates intent.

WARNING: Do NOT restructure the useEffect in App.tsx. The only change needed is stabilizing the `handleNotification` reference in useServerNotifications.ts. The `[handleNotification]` dep in App.tsx is correct — it just needs to stop changing on every render.

Files to modify:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/hooks/useServerNotifications.ts (lines 49-55 and 57-152: wrap both functions in useCallback)
